author: @MichaelQShaw
text:
Constant shifting center of mass. Forces produce energy. The force of explosions power your internal combustion engine vehicle, not thermal heat transfer. Heat is a byproduct of the chemical reaction of burning fuels. 

Constant shifting center of mass. Wind is a force in one direction, gravity is also. The very fact that the center of mass can fall outside of an object makes this possible.

replies.author: @MichaelQShaw
replies.text:
Please consider, hydro power. If the constant downward force of gravity providing hydraulic head, did not exist or was not present, there would be no hydroelectric power at all.

Even so power generated by waves then is also power generated by gravity because it is the interactions of gravity, the physical water, the rotation of the earth, the orbit of the moon, and gravity generating waves. 

So it is power generated by the force of gravity and the Earths’ physical motion. Again if the constant downward force of gravity were not present or the Earth and moon were motionless wave energy would not exist as well as hydro generated power 

I realize we are not astrophysicists like Neil deGrasse Tyson, and I realize that hydro power slows the rotation of the Earth to a negligible degree. My only argument is that it is the very presence of gravity AND the motion of the water/Earth/moon that produces the energy.

Thought experiment…

If the Earth was not rotating on its axis and was locked with one face always facing the sun in its orbit around the sun (just as the moon is).

And the moon also locked with only one side facing the Earth as it is now.

And the moon did not orbit the Earth and was stationary in one position on the opposite side of the Earth away from the sun…

There would be no tide, only a ring like bulge of water on both sides of the Earths oceans. Possibly with the larger bulge being on the side facing the sun. 

Like a standing wave, and “tidal power” would not be possible. Even if the Earth still continues to orbit the sun.

So bottom line, in hydro power it takes the thermal energy of the sun to replenish the water, the water itself and the present force of gravity to produce energy.

With tidal power it takes the kinetic motion and the present force of gravity to produce energy.

In my design, it takes the downward force of gravity, the benign normal force, imbalance intentionally provided by its design, and a swinging pendulum like shifting center of gravity off to one side. 

We can produce energy from forces. We do it already.

I expect nobody to believe me.

text: Except for experts like Howard Johnson and people like Stan Meyer and Paul Pantone. I know many will claim conspiracy theory but I speak truth from these inventors, for these inventors and for the people.
